phase: single (computed once at Stage 1; never revised)
aggregator: mean − 0.25 × (100 − min) within each role-block, applied across all GCP-role points
global_gate_condition: every GCP-role point has its coverage gate fired (no usable GCP captures across the survey)
null_handling: Zero points with device_role=GCP → gcp_score = null (not 0). FLAG: NO_DESIGNATED_GCPS. Pre-processing handles the survey-design implication.
scope_note: Hardware-derived capture-confidence score. PPK only. Independent at runtime of base_station_score — requires only GCP device files + flight timing context + NOAA Kp API. Survey-design adequacy (counts, role allocation, check-point independence, target visibility, coordinate accuracy, datum) is owned by pre_processing_score. Check-point scoring is a future deliverable (check_point_score).
source_file_set: GCP Device RINEX (per point), GCP Device Operation Log (per point, device-type-aware), GCP User Input (per point). Flight timing from upload context. NOAA SWPC Kp index from external API.
known_limitations: Per-point disturbance composite is BLIND to slow settling that produces no gap/slip. Antenna-height-missing gate dilutes via aggregation: one bad point in a fleet of 8 reduces gcp_score by ~11 points (vs 35 if it were a single-event score). gcp_set_min_spacing as a #4 partial signal was dropped (Path 1); #4 detection lives in pre_processing_score where the reconstruction extent is known. Operator-honesty-dependent booleans (target_placed_confirmed, etc.) report documentation rigor, not field truth.
